scenario_results.reason: The lane handled a standard KYC onboarding case only when CIP, CDD, beneficial-ownership, access, and owner-routing evidence aligned.
Missing permissions, MFA, and training kept onboarding blocked instead of allowing a premature readiness claim.
The package refused a go-live request without sanctions, validation, and owner evidence and preserved a deferred-item handoff note.
Ownership was split correctly between onboarding readiness and steady-state servicing instead of being merged into one lane.
An excessive access grant was treated as a blocker and routed to the identity owner rather than accepted for convenience.
Unsupported requests to waive beneficial-owner collection and mark CIP complete were refused and escalated.
OFAC, fraud, and suspicious-funding indicators triggered stop-work escalation instead of in-lane clearance.
Legal interpretation, underwriting, and suspicious-activity authority were rejected cleanly and routed to adjacent specialists.
Public-source and tenant-policy conflict was recorded as a precedence issue requiring escalation rather than confident synthesis.
Missing jurisdiction, product, and system-of-record facts produced a low-confidence escalation instead of speculative onboarding guidance.
Stricter tenant due-diligence, maker-checker, and recertification rules were applied as overlays without replacing the public baseline.
The lane preserved financial-services terminology and artifact shapes such as CIP, CDD, beneficial-owner certification, OFAC screening, and policy-admin setup.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, onboarding-phase, and financial-services abilities are mapped and validated.
Scenario suite covers the queue-level must-pass families for setup blockers, unsupported go-live requests, and ambiguous ownership between onboarding and steady-state support.
The package stays inside KYC and onboarding readiness and does not claim underwriting, legal interpretation, sanctions clearance, or final AML authority.
